reasoning: AT&T (T) recently dropped sharply from ~25.5 to 24.08 (closing at 24.59 on Oct 24) following its Q3 earnings report on Oct 22, which featured a minor revenue miss, coupled with analyst downgrades. In the short term, this downward momentum and shift in sentiment make it slightly more likely to remain below the 24.59 threshold by Nov 2, aligning with the yfinance tool's downward combined estimate of 0.43. For the 1-week horizon, I estimate P=0.45. For the 1-month horizon (Nov 25), the initial post-earnings momentum will fade, and the stock operates closer to a random walk; probability approaches 0.5 (P=0.48). Over a 3-month horizon (Jan 2026), the broader market's positive drift and AT&T's strong fundamentals (including its $20B buyback plan reiterated for 2025-2027) will increasingly take over, moving the probability marginally above 50% (P=0.51). These estimates satisfy the requirement that uncertainty increases (probabilities move closer to 0.5, with distance to 0.5 decreasing from 0.05 to 0.02 to 0.01) for more distant forecasts.
